name: Graph Basics
description: A graph stores vertices and edges; adjacency lists are the standard efficient representation.
commonMistakes: Forgetting whether the graph is directed or undirected
Using an adjacency matrix unnecessarily for sparse graphs

name: Adjacency List
description: Store each node's outgoing neighbors in a list for efficient traversal.
commonMistakes: Not adding the reverse edge in an undirected graph
Indexing the graph incorrectly

name: DFS on Graphs
description: Explore depth-first using recursion or an explicit stack while marking visited nodes.
exampleJava: visited[node] = true; for (int nei : graph.get(node)) dfs(nei);
commonMistakes: Forgetting visited checks and revisiting forever
Assuming one DFS call covers all components automatically

name: BFS on Graphs
description: Use a queue to explore breadth-first and discover minimum-edge distances in unweighted graphs.
commonMistakes: Marking visited too late and pushing duplicates
Using DFS when shortest edge-count path is needed

name: Connected Components
description: Run DFS or BFS from each unvisited node to count disconnected pieces of the graph.
exampleJava: for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) if (!visited[i]) { dfs(i); components++; }
commonMistakes: Only traversing from node 0
Not resetting logic across components

name: Cycle Detection
description: Use parent tracking in undirected graphs or recursion state/indegree logic in directed graphs.
commonMistakes: Treating the parent edge like a cycle in undirected graphs
Using undirected logic on directed graphs

name: Grid BFS
description: Treat each cell as a graph node and use BFS with direction vectors.
commonMistakes: Going out of bounds
Not marking visited cells correctly

name: Topological Sort
description: Order nodes of a DAG so every directed edge points forward in the ordering.
commonMistakes: Applying topo sort to graphs with cycles without checking
Forgetting to initialize indegrees correctly

name: Unweighted Shortest Path
description: BFS gives the minimum number of edges from a source in an unweighted graph.
commonMistakes: Using DFS and expecting minimum distance
Updating distances after revisiting nodes unnecessarily

name: Dijkstra Algorithm
description: Use a min-priority queue to relax weighted edges in graphs with non-negative weights.
commonMistakes: Using Dijkstra with negative weights
Not skipping stale priority queue entries

name: Disjoint Set Union
description: Track connectivity with find and union operations using path compression and union by rank/size.
commonMistakes: Forgetting path compression
Not checking whether two nodes are already in the same set

name: Minimum Spanning Tree
description: Build the lightest set of edges connecting all nodes without cycles.
exampleJava: if (find(u) != find(v)) { union(u, v); cost += w; }
commonMistakes: Adding an edge that forms a cycle
Sorting or selecting edges incorrectly
